reasoning: I vote for didi's party—always have, always will. What matters most is that she has promised no NRC in Bengal, and she keeps that word. I have my land records, my family has been here for generations, but I need a government that does not make me prove I belong to my own land. The BJP channels talk about Hindu refugees and infiltrators; Shantanu Thakur's politics is not for people like me. Yes, I heard about Sandeshkhali—bad things, police went—but one man's crime does not make the whole government wrong. Didi still says no NRC. That is what keeps me and my family safe. The Krishak Bandhu money came because of her, the two-rupee rice comes because of her. I am settled in this choice.
primary_drivers: Mamata's no-NRC commitment—my core security against being asked to prove I belong
AITC farmer schemes (Krishak Bandhu, Khadya Sathi ration card)
Distrust of BJP's focus on Hindu refugees and the way they speak of Muslims as outsiders
